title: Latent Potential Parable
html:
Stub. Yahir's dad told him a parable on 2021-04-18 about an 18-year-old applying for a circus job. The circus manager offers him sweeping/mopping work; the kid says "I can imitate birds" — and then mid-sentence flies away. The lesson: don't measure unknown people by the buckets you have available. Source: 2021 04 19 circus parable.

The parable is uncharacteristically tight for the corpus — most of Yahir's notes are sprawling. Worth densifying if other dad-stories surface.

related.label: Circus parable — 18-year-old who 'imitates birds' and flies away
